prompt_text: Render vs Railway pricing and features
response:
# Render vs Railway: Pricing & Features Comparison

## **Pricing**

| Feature | Render | Railway |
|---------|--------|------|
| **Free Tier** | Yes (limited) | Yes (limited) |
| **Compute Cost** | $0.10/hour per instance | $0.50/GB RAM/month base |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL/MySQL included | PostgreSQL/MySQL available |
| **Minimum** | $7/month (after free tier) | Pay-as-you-go (~$5/month typical) |
| **Free Credits** | $7/month permanent | $5 startup credit only |

## **Key Features**

### **Render**
| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Languages** | Node, Python, Ruby, Go, Java, Rust |
| **Databases** | PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis |
| **Scaling** | Manual + basic auto-scaling |
| **Deploys** | From Git (GitHub, GitLab) |
| **SSL/TLS** | Free, automatic |
| **Environment** | Managed containers |
| **Free Tier Limitation** | Spins down after 15 min inactivity |

### **Railway**
| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Languages** | Broader: supports anything with Dockerfile |
| **Databases** | PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis |
| **Scaling** | More automatic, flexible |
| **Deploys** | Git + direct Docker/CLI push |
| **SSL/TLS** | Free, automatic |
| **Environment** | Container-based |
| **Free Tier Limitation** | $5 credit, then charges immediately |

## **Pros & Cons**

### **Render Pros**
✅ Clear, predictable pricing  
✅ $7/month permanent free tier  
✅ Easier for beginners  
✅ Better documentation for common use cases  

### **Render Cons**
❌ Free tier apps sleep (slow startup)  
❌ Less flexible scaling options  
❌ Smaller ecosystem  

### **Railway Pros**
✅ More powerful free tier ($5 credit)  
✅ Better auto-scaling  
✅ More flexible deployment options  
✅ Supports more databases (MongoDB)  
✅ Better for complex applications  

### **Railway Cons**
❌ No permanent free tier (credits expire)  
❌ Steeper learning curve  
❌ Faster billing once credits end  

## **Best For**

| Choose **Render** if you want | Choose **Railway** if you want |
|------|------|
| Simple hobby projects | Complex applications |
| Permanent free tier | Maximum flexibility |
| Predictable costs | Automatic scaling |
| Easy onboarding | Docker support |

## **Verdict**
- **Beginners:** Render (simpler, free tier with no sleep)
- **Serious Projects:** Railway (better scaling, more features)
- **Cost-conscious:** Render (predictable $7/month minimum)

Both are far superior to Heroku post-free tier shutdown.
